keyFacts: Golden cookies grant semi-random effects that usually improve production or cookie gain.
Golden cookie effects can stack as of v2.002; repeated active effects add to their timers.
Lucky gains are capped by the lower of banked cookies and CpS-based production, so banking can matter for Lucky strategy.

outcomes.name: Dragon Harvest
outcomes.requirement: requires active Reaper of Fields and/or Reality Bending aura, RB has chance/10

outcomes.name: Dragonflight
outcomes.requirement: requires active Dragonflight and/or Reality Bending aura, RB has chance/10

__summary.timing: There are three crucial timings for Golden Cookies: Spawn time: how long it takes for Golden Cookies to be generated. Duration: how long Golden Cookies will stay on the screen. Effect duration: how long the cookie's effects will last. Spawn Time Golden Cookie spawning is treated like any other object, and is thus subject to the game's built-in spawning mechanism. The time between one cookie spawn and the next is always between the 2 numbers Tmin and Tmax (which are initially set at 300 and 900 seconds, respectively). During this interval, there is a ( t - T min T max - T min ) 5 {\displaystyle \left({\frac {t-T_{\text{min}}}{T_{\text{max}}-T_{\text{min}}}}\right)^{5}} chance for a golden cookie to spawn each frame. (The default frame rate is 30 FPS, or 30 frames per second.) The probability of a golden cookie spawning can be approximated by the following functions: p ( t ) = 30 t 5 T 5 exp ( - 5 t 6 T 5 ) {\displaystyle p(t)={\frac {30t^{5}}{T^{5}}}\exp \left({\frac {-5t^{6}}{T^{5}}}\right)} P ( t ) = 1 - exp ( - 5 t 6 T 5 ) {\displaystyle P(t)=1-\exp \left({\frac {-5t^{6}}{T^{5}}}\right)} p ( t ) = p ( t - T min ) between T min and T max {\displaystyle {\bar {p}}(t)=p(t-T_{\text{min}})\quad {\text{between }}T_{\text{min}}{\text{ and }}T_{\text{max}}} P ( t ) = P ( t - T min ) between T min and T max {\displaystyle {\bar {P}}(t)=P(t-T_{\text{min}})\quad {\text{between }}T_{\...
__summary.strategy: The Golden Cookie may fail to spawn the first time you start a new game on a browser with no saved Cookie Clicker data. This happens if minTime and maxTime do not get set properly. If you are going for a "true neverclick" run, reload Cookie Clicker after "Game saved" first appears (alternatively, save manually by pressing Ctrl+S). Golden Cookies are more visible against the black background of the secondary windows in Cookie Clicker (Stats, Options, Info). If you've been waiting for a long time for a Golden Cookie, click into Stats. This will also make it impossible to accidentally perform actions such as selling stocks or harvesting crops while chasing down a fading cookie or during a Cookie Storm. Before obtaining the Golden Cookie upgrades you can time roughly when the next one will appear based on when you clicked on the last one. The minimum time between each is 5 minutes without upgrades. This makes getting the 7 and 27 clicks needed to buy Lucky Day and Serendipity easier. After you get the upgrades there's less of a need to time them as they appear frequently. With both upgrades, if you get a Frenzy outcome, the earliest time a new Golden Cookie can spawn is thirty seconds before the frenzy ends. By keeping a large sum of cookies in your bank, at any given point you can maximize the returns of the Lucky outcome. The maximum available from a Lucky outcome is 900 times...
__summary.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

__meta.schema: Golden cookie outcomes plus related upgrades/achievements keyed by name. Metadata keys begin with __.

Lucky!.Name: Lucky!
details: Instantly gain cookies, whichever gives less:
Will give the most cookies if cookies baked is 100 minutes (1 hour 40 mins) of CpS
Will scale accordingly with CpS-altering effects (except Wrinklers)
You can get more cookies if you have other effects like Frenzy and Building Special
Lucky!.description: Instantly gain cookies, whichever gives less: 15% of cookies banked + 13 15 minutes worth of cookies (CpS * 900) + 13 Will give the most cookies if cookies baked is 100 minutes (1 hour 40 mins) of CpS
Lucky!.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Frenzy.Name: Frenzy
Frenzy.description: x7 CpS Lasts 77 seconds Stacks with Building Special
Frenzy.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Building special.Name: Building special
details: Random building will be picked, each boosting CpS by 10% (N * 10%, where N is building count)
Must have 10 or more of a building for it to be eligible
Each building's effect is unique, so multiple of different buildings can be active at once
Names of every type of building special:
Building special.description: Random building will be picked, each boosting CpS by 10% (N * 10%, where N is building count) Lasts 30 seconds Must have 10 or more of a building for it to be eligible Each building's effect is unique, so multiple of different buildings can be active at once
Building special.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Cookie Storm.Name: Cookie Storm
details: Spawns lots of Golden/Wrath cookies randomly on-screen, each giving 1-7 minutes of CpS
Golden/Wrath cookies from storm will not increment 'Golden cookies clicks' stat (and subsequently achievements)
Random drops can still be unlocked
Cookie Storm.description: Spawns lots of Golden/Wrath cookies randomly on-screen, each giving 1-7 minutes of CpS Lasts 7 seconds Golden/Wrath cookies from storm will not increment 'Golden cookies clicks' stat (and subsequently achievements) Random drops can still be unlocked
Cookie Storm.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Cookie chain.Name: Cookie chain
details: Each golden cookie clicked after the first will give x10 + 7 more cookies than the last, until chain ends
If in the Grandmapocalypse, or if Skruuia is slotted, Wrath Cookies may (or will outright) spawn in place of Golden Cookies, giving x10 + 6 more cookies than the last Golden/Wrath cookie
During chain, subsequent Golden/Wrath cookies spawn every 3 seconds

If the GC starting the chain is the one spawned by DEoRL and the natural GC has been clicked, then the next GC timer has already been set, so the chain will continue when the next GC is clicked. If any non-natural GC is clicked during this time, then the chain will break.

If the GC starting the chain is the one spawned by DEoRL and the natural GC has not been clicked, then the other cookie will continue the chain and set the timer correctly, This will result in a normal chain after the natural GC is clicked. (It can be delayed by waiting while the natural GC is on screen.)
Starting from 10 billion cookies, Golden/Wrath Cookies that trigger Cookie Chain will start at higher orders of magnitude (x100, x1000, etc)
Every x10 more cookies will increment the order of magnitude
Chain will end under these circumstances:
A Golden/Wrath Cookie disappears before being clicked
Next Golden/Wrath cookie would give more than 6 hours worth of CpS
Next Golden/Wrath cookie would give more than 50% of cookies banked
1% chance after clicking a Golden/Wrath Cookie
Clicking the Golden/Wrath Cookie spawned by Force the Hand of Fate in Grimoire
Cookie chain.description: Each golden cookie clicked after the first will give x10 + 7 more cookies than the last, until chain ends If in the Grandmapocalypse, or if Skruuia is slotted, Wrath Cookies may (or will outright) spawn in place of Golden Cookies, giving x10 + 6 more cookies than the last Golden/Wrath cookie During chain, subsequent Golden/Wrath cookies spawn every 3 seconds If the GC starting the chain is the one spawned by DEoRL and the natural GC has been clicked, then the next GC timer has already been set, so the chain will continue when the next GC is clicked. If any non-natural GC is clicked during this time, then the chain will break.
Cookie chain.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Sweet.Name: Sweet
Sweet.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Blab.Name: Blab
details: Does absolutely nothing except for displaying one of the following messages:
Golden Cookie shininess doubled for 3 seconds!
Fooled you! This one was just a test.
Your click has been registered. Thank you for your cooperation.
Thank you. A team has been dispatched.
Oops. This was just a chocolate cookie with shiny aluminium foil.
Wait, sorry, I wasn't ready yet.
Huh? Oh, there was nothing there.
It seems you hallucinated that cookie.
This golden cookie was a complete fabrication.
In theory there is no wrong way of clicking a golden cookie, but you did that, somehow.
All cookies multiplied by 999! All cookies divided by 999!
Blab.description: Does absolutely nothing except for displaying one of the following messages: Cookie crumbliness x3 for 60 seconds! Chocolatiness x7 for 77 seconds! Dough elasticity halved for 66 seconds!
Blab.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Lucky day.Name: Lucky day
Lucky day.description: Golden cookies appear twice as often and last twice as long on screen. "Oh hey, a four-leaf penny!"
Lucky day.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Serendipity.Name: Serendipity
Serendipity.description: Golden cookies appear twice as often and last twice as long on screen. "What joy! Seven horseshoes!"

Golden goose egg.Name: Golden goose egg
Golden goose egg.description: Golden cookies appear 5% more often. Cost scales with how many eggs you own. "The sole vestige of a tragic tale involving misguided investments."
Golden goose egg.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Green yeast digestives.Name: Green yeast digestives
Green yeast digestives.unlock condition: 0.5% chance of dropping after harvesting a mature green rot [note 2]
Green yeast digestives.description: +1% golden cookie gains and effect duration. +1% golden cookie frequency. +3% random drops. Dropped by green rot plants. Cost scales with CpS. "These are tastier than you'd expect, but not by much."
Green yeast digestives.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text

Dragon fang.Name: Dragon fang
Dragon fang.base price: Money 1,800 x CpS (x0.1 if max level dragon)
Dragon fang.description: +3% golden cookie gains. Dragon harvest and Dragonflight are 10% stronger. Cost scales with CpS, but 10 times cheaper with a fully-trained dragon. "Just a fallen baby tooth your dragon wanted you to have, as a gift. It might be smaller than an adult tooth, but it's still frighteningly sharp - and displays some awe-inspiring cavities, which you might expect from a creature made out of sweets."
Dragon fang.source: Cookie Clicker Wiki Golden Cookie pasted text
